_comment: Tunables for how the companion FEELS. Distances are in pixels, speeds in px/sec, intervals in seconds. 'personality' is the legacy 0..1 fallback; 'traits' are the live, drifting versions seeded from it. 'drift' controls how slowly the companion comes to reflect how you play. Tune all of this to change the vibe without touching code.
_follow_comment: The companion's comfortable distance TIGHTENS as the bond grows. Fresh, it keeps a wide berth (follow_near_low) — its own creature, free to be anywhere, with little pull to trail you; the camera is locked to the player, so a fresh companion will often be off-screen, living its own life. As you bond this shrinks to the snug at-your-side follow_near. follow_near_curve shapes that shrink: <1 makes it tighten EARLY and steadily so the closing-in is felt across the whole arc (not a late snap). follow_deadzone is a comfort BUBBLE past the comfort distance inside which Follow only pulls gently — so a bonded companion mills about within the bubble instead of rigidly snapping to one trailing point (it never feels fully leashed). run_distance is the separate walk->run threshold: how far behind the companion must fall before it hustles to catch up (so a bonded companion can still keep up with a moving player).
_curiosity_consider_comment: The beat of HESITATION before coming over to something the player interacted with. Noticing is instant (it perks and looks); deciding to walk over is a considered choice. delay_low/high: seconds it pauses to weigh it up, by bond — longer when fresh, brief (but never zero) when bonded, so coming over always reads as a live decision. approach_low/high: the probability it actually goes, by bond — fresh companions often stay put, a fully bonded one always comes (after the slight delay). appeal_weight, valence_weight, arousal_weight: small nudges so bond stays the dominant axis — a tempting find (high appraised appeal, centered on appraisal.neutral) or a bright/energized mood makes it readier to go; a dull thing or a withdrawn mood, less so. Mood's random walk is what gives the same prop at the same bond its day-to-day variability.
_wander_comment: Self-directed roaming — the independent, pre-bond life, but it never fully stops. Left alone the companion moseys to a spot, lingers, then after a pause picks somewhere new. wander_radius: how near a prop must be to be chosen as a destination (it investigates props it passes). roam_radius: how far an aimless amble strays when there's no prop to head for — set to map scale so a fresh companion can end up anywhere on the map (it shrinks on its own as the bond grows). wander_min_radius: a FLOOR on that range so even a fully bonded companion still takes visible little excursions around you rather than gluing to your side. roam_pause: [min,max] seconds of standing about between roams. roam_pause_bond_scale: pauses are multiplied toward this as bond->1, so a bonded companion ranges less. wander_linger: seconds it dwells once it arrives.
_wander_stuck_comment: Safety valve for a roam that picks a target it physically can't reach (beyond the border treeline, a solid prop, or off the map edge — the companion has no path-finding). If it means to walk yet moves less than wander_stuck_fraction of the expected per-frame distance for wander_stuck_time seconds, it judges the way blocked, abandons the roam, and pauses — rather than grinding against the barrier forever.
_bond_comment: The relationship itself (0..1), persistent across sessions. It starts at 'init' (a fresh companion is its own creature) and grows ONLY through genuine play — it is deliberately un-grindable. Two sources: (1) grow_per_interaction on each shared examine, NOVELTY-weighted — the first encounter with a given prop pays full, each repeat is multiplied by novelty_decay^(times_seen) so it fades to ~0 (you can't farm bond by poking one thing; familiarity is permanent and never fades). (2) grow_per_shared_attention on a SHARED-ATTENTION moment — the companion is right beside whatever you're focused on (from social referencing) — also novelty-weighted in the same familiarity map. (3) grow_per_area the first time it reaches a named region of the world (or a new world) — BINARY, not decayed: a place you've been earns nothing on return. (4) grow_per_sec_near, a slow proximity TRICKLE while you stay within the (bond-scaled) comfort distance — the gentle long-tail finisher, kept weak so parking next to the companion is never the optimal play. Raw presence growth (just from the game running) was removed as farmable. novelty_decay in (0,1): lower = props go quiet faster. The base rates are calibrated for the REAL game: genuine play reaches a fairly close bond in roughly 10 hours, front-loaded on discovery then tapering to the trickle. 'time_scale' multiplies all growth so you can experience the full fresh->bonded transformation quickly while tuning feel — at 145 a full bond takes a few minutes. Set time_scale back to 1.0 for the real game. Bond is saved, so a returning companion already knows you.
_expression_comment: How the companion's FEELING shows in its body — the bridge from the mood sim to something the player can read without numbers. Pure presentation tuning (CompanionView maps the eased mood through this, VectorActor draws it). ease_rate: per-second smoothing of mood toward the brain's value, so body language glides rather than twitches. tail_wag_rate [calm,excited]: wag cycles/sec by arousal. tail_wag_amp: max sideways wag, reached only at high valence (a still/tucked tail when withdrawn) — the clearest 'glad you're here' tell. ear_raise: how far ears perk UP at peak happiness; ear_droop: how far they sag at low valence. idle_bounce_gain [calm,excited]: multiplies the resting breathing bob by arousal, so an excited companion fidgets and a calm one settles. emote_life: seconds a floating emote glyph lives.
_mood_comment: The fast affective layer — a 2D feeling that overlays the slower traits and gives each day its own weather. valence (withdrawn..warm) rides on clinginess; arousal (calm..energized) rides on energy; curiosity has no mood axis. overlay_scale: how strongly mood bends the effective trait (every action reads traits through CompanionTraits, so this biases the whole decision, not just the look). relax_rate: per-second ease back toward the resting point, so event spikes fade over a few seconds. rest_valence/rest_arousal: the resting point is derived from a trait via lerp(lo,hi,trait) — an energetic companion rests at higher arousal, a warm one at higher valence — so different companions feel different even at rest. discovery_*: a novel examine spikes valence+arousal, multiplied by the prop's novelty (a habituated prop barely moves it). separation_valence_rate: valence eases down per second while the player is far, times bond (a fresh companion likes its independence; a bonded one misses you). contagion_*: a moving player pulls arousal up (gain * player_speed/ref_speed, lightly bond-scaled). boredom_*: after boredom_onset seconds with nothing novel, arousal drifts down so the next novel thing pops. shared_attention_valence: a warming valence lift on a shared-attention moment (you and it focused on the same thing), novelty-weighted. noticed_valence_gain: a gentle continuous valence lift while the player seems to be noticing the companion (noticed_strength * this * delta). walk_amp: amplitude of the per-frame random walk — the PRIMARY source of day-to-day variety. neg_floor: cozy asymmetry — mood can't drop below this mild floor (real lows arrive with danger later). axis_map: which trait each mood axis overlays.
_attention_comment: SOCIAL REFERENCING: how the companion reads what the PLAYER is focused on, and how strongly it takes that as a cue. It infers 'you're attending to X' from the player being near + slow + (approaching X or having lingered near it). radius: how near the player must be to a prop for it to be a candidate. slow_speed: player speed (px/s) at/above which 'slowness' reads as 0 (so attention only forms when you ease off). slow_gate: minimum slowness before dwell starts accumulating. dwell_full: seconds of lingering to reach full dwell — this is the believable BEAT OF LATENCY (it catches on a moment after you settle). dwell_decay: how fast dwell drains once you move on. approach_floor: engagement when you're near+slow but neither approaching nor dwelling (keep low). min_strength: below this the signal is ignored. hold/switch_margin: hysteresis so the gaze doesn't flicker between two nearby props. RESPONSE: glance_floor is the LOW BOND FLOOR for glancing at what you peer at — even a fresh companion does it sometimes (chance = strength * lerp(glance_floor,1,bond) * curiosity * glance_gain). cue_weight scales the BOND-GATED chance it actually wanders over to investigate alongside you (chance = bond * strength * cue_weight) — 0 when fresh, growing as you bond. SHARED-ATTENTION (feeds bond + mood): a shared moment is counted when attention_strength >= share_min_strength AND the companion is within share_distance of what you're attending to. BEING-NOTICED (feeds mood): notice_radius is how near the player must be to the companion to possibly be 'noticing' it; notice_floor is the engagement when near+slow but not approaching the companion (keep 0 so only turning-toward-and-coming-over reads as noticing).
_appraisal_comment: APPRAISAL turns a thing's neutral 'tags' (authored in world.json) into how drawn THIS companion is to it (0..1 'appeal'). affinities are the creature's tastes per tag (0.5 neutral, >0.5 likes, <0.5 indifferent); an unknown tag and an untagged thing read 'neutral'. appeal = mean(tag affinities) * lerp(curiosity_lo, curiosity_hi, curiosity), so a curious companion finds a little more to like in everything. Effects wired so far: a high-appeal prop gives a bigger mood delight spike on examine, and the companion lingers longer admiring it (linger_appeal scales curiosity_linger by appeal). Tastes are species-shared for now (per-companion favourites are a later refinement).
_checkin_comment: Self-initiated visits: while off living its own life, the companion now and then wanders over to say hi — the 'oh, what's this?' / 'there it is again!' beat — then heads back to what it was doing. It only fires when it isn't already close (dist past the comfort distance) and the player is within follow_far. checkin_score is what an active visit bids: above wander and low-bond follow, below a player-triggered look. checkin_interval [min,max] seconds is the wall-clock spacing between visits (NOT scaled by bond time_scale, so a handful land in a short dev session). checkin_pull_low..high is how eager it is to visit by bond — high when fresh and far, fading to ~0 once bonded (by then it's already close, so visits aren't needed). checkin_linger: seconds it hangs out once it arrives. checkin_stop_distance: how close it comes before settling in to say hi.
_come_comment: The player's CALL / whistle — a bid for attention, never a guaranteed summon. It depends on BOTH distance and bond. hear_radius: the companion only hears a call within this range; whistle from farther and nothing happens (no infinite-range summon — if it has wandered off, you may have to go find it). When it does hear, it first ACKNOWLEDGES: it stands, looks over and perks for ack_pause seconds (a beat that reads as 'I heard you'), with a small mood lift (ack_valence/ack_arousal) from being called — being noticed by you feels good even if it doesn't come. Then, based on BOND, it either goes back to its own life or comes running: chance_low..chance_high is the probability it comes, by bond (a fresh companion rarely comes, a fully bonded one always does), nudged a little by mood (valence_weight/arousal_weight). If it comes, it runs to within stop_distance and gives a happy arrival hop. Calling never grows bond (a whistle isn't earned discovery) — only the relationship makes the call reliable, which is the whole point.
_pet_comment: Deliberate affection, available only when you're adjacent (the Pet affordance fades in within range). What the pet DOES depends on BOND. range: the brain only accepts a pet within this distance (matches the controller's PET_RANGE). accept_low..accept_high: the probability it welcomes the pet, by bond — a fresh, wary companion (accept_low) often SHIES a little away instead (a startle step back, no bond, a tiny mood dip that never drops below the cozy floor); a bonded one (accept_high) always leans in. step: how far it leans toward you on a good pet, or shies back on a rebuff (px). On acceptance: a warm mood lift (valence/arousal) and a small bond gain, gated TWICE so it can't be farmed — novelty-weighted in the familiarity map under 'pet' (first pets pay, repeats fade) AND only once per bond_cooldown seconds of play time. rebuff_valence: the small mood dip on a shy-away (negative, clamped to neg_floor). duration: seconds the beat lasts. So petting a stranger is a gamble; petting a friend is reliably sweet — the relationship is, again, the mechanic.
_lead_comment: Companion-LED discovery — the deepest partnership beat, where the companion stops being a reactor and takes YOU somewhere. On its own initiative it picks an appealing, still-novel prop and leads you to it, glancing back to check you're following, then presents it with a delight and a shared-discovery bond/mood bump. Reserved for a FULLY bonded companion (min_bond, set to bond.max): leading you to share a find is something only a partner that completely trusts you does. On the social band with a CheckIn-like spacing. interval: seconds between attempts (deterministic, NOT rng — a lead is a considered, no-dice beat; also the initial delay so it doesn't fire on spawn). min_appeal: only leads to a prop it actually likes (appraised appeal). min_novelty: and only one still fresh — never drags you to something you've already examined together (novelty is keyed by the same prop id as Examine, so the two paths never double-pay). beckon_time: the 'come on!' beat before setting off. glance_interval/glance_pause: how often, and for how long, it stops to look back and check you're coming. follow_check_distance/patience: if you lag beyond follow_check_distance for patience seconds it gives up gracefully and goes back to its life. stop_distance: how close to the prop it leads before presenting. present_linger: how long it admires the find with you before the beat ends.
_balance_comment: How bond tips the wander-vs-follow contest. Self-wander scores wander_score_low at bond 0 (its own agenda) fading to wander_score_high at bond 1 (kept above 0 so a bonded companion still potters). Following scores follow_eager_low..follow_eager_high by bond, PLUS a distance leash (0 at the comfort distance ramping to follow_leash at follow_far), and is scaled by the clinginess trait. follow_eager_low is 0 and the comfort distance is wide when fresh, so a new companion has little pull to follow; the leash only comes to matter as the bond deepens and the comfort distance tightens in. A player-triggered look is fixed well above all of these.
_trait_spread_comment: trait_spread is how far a freshly CREATED or RESET companion's traits are randomly jittered around their init, so playthroughs differ gently (a touch more wander-inclined, interactable-inclined, or follower-y) without becoming pronounced archetypes. A per-trait 'spread' inside an entry below overrides this default for that trait. Does not affect the deterministic make_default path the tests use.

traits._comment: init must match personality above so first-run feel is unchanged. min/max bound how far a trait can ever go; drift_rate is how fast IDENTITY learns toward play style (per second toward target) — keep it small so change is felt, not seen.

drift._comment: Controls how the slow IDENTITY anchor learns toward how you play. warmup_seconds: watch this long before learning at all (so it doesn't lock onto noisy opening seconds). reference_* set what counts as 'a lot' of roaming / examining. targets: weighted blend of play signals (explore, together, engage) each trait's identity learns toward. birth_residual: how far the learning target is pulled back toward the BIRTH inclination (0 = becomes a pure mirror of play style; higher = keeps more individuality) — also what stops a companion getting stuck by an unlucky birth. crystallize_exp: identity's learning rate is multiplied by (1-bond)^crystallize_exp, so it's malleable when fresh and LOCKS as the bond deepens (higher = locks sooner). The disposition layer (below) is what behavior reads.

_disposition_comment: The live DISPOSITION layer — the value behavior actually reads — orbits the identity anchor. regress_rate: per-second pull back toward identity (so any lingering push, like a future 'upset', relaxes away on its own). band: how far disposition may sit from identity (identity +/- band), the room a mood/event has to bend the current personality without rewriting who it is. With no events in the cozy slice, disposition simply tracks identity.

arbiter._comment: How competing actions are ranked. Each action sits in a priority BAND; a higher band always wins over a lower one (so structure, not a hand-tuned big number, makes Investigate interrupt). Desire only decides things WITHIN a band — that's where Follow vs Wander vs Idle is settled, on the magnitudes tuned above. COMMITMENT is the continuous within-band resistance the running action adds to its own desire (it never crosses bands): commit_bonus is the small nudge every running action carries so a marginally-keener rival can't cause flip-flop jitter at a crossover (raise = more stubborn, lower = more reactive); committed_inertia is the extra a COMMITTED beat (a roam underway, a visit, a look) adds on top so it finishes rather than being yanked — it's deliberately larger than any same-band rival's plausible desire, so a committed beat is released only by its own state (e.g. a roam giving up its target), never by a desire crossover (which would just re-form the jitter at a higher threshold). Grading preemption ACROSS bands is a danger-era concern, not used in the cozy slice.

bands._comment: autonomous = Follow/Wander/Idle (its own life). social = CheckIn (a chosen visit beats its own life). interrupt = Investigate (a player-triggered look beats everything autonomous). task and command are RESERVED SEAMS, not used yet: 'task' is for future multi-step chores (cook/farm/errand) that should outrank idle living but yield to a look or an order; 'command' is for future player-issued orders that preempt all. Leaving the integers spaced lets new bands slot between without renumbering.

actions._comment: Per-action declarative desire. 'considerations' are IAUS axes — each reads a normalized 0..1 input and bends it through a response curve (linear/power/logistic/step, optional invert). Author an action's appeal here instead of in code. idle.weight is its flat baseline desire.

checkin._comment: Chance it bothers to visit = product of these axes (pull fades with bond, keener when farther, scaled by how sociable it is). Reshape any axis here — e.g. make distance a power curve so it only visits when quite far — without touching code.
